issue.title: Rust behavior-guard tests: serde IPC round-trip per struct + ban raw Command::new

successCriteria.description: A cargo serde round-trip test covers every IPC-returned struct, asserting expected camelCase keys are present; it fails if a field is dropped or mis-renamed on the wire
skip_serializing_if fields asserted present-when-Some / absent-when-None; non-optional + #[serde(default)] fields asserted always-present (the #259 invariant)
A source-scan guard bans raw Command::new outside hidden_command (github/mod.rs); demonstrated to match a planted bypass string, not just the wrapper definition; portable-pty not falsely flagged
cargo --lib + full pnpm test green; the existing #259 tiki_release_archived_survives_serialization assertion is preserved (kept or folded with the same check)

phases.title: Serde IPC round-trip test for every command-returned struct
verification: cargo --lib round-trip test covers all IPC-returned structs with camelCase key assertions
skip_serializing_if Some/None both asserted; default/non-optional always-present asserted
WorkContext union tag asserted; #259 archived assertion preserved
phases.summary: Added #[cfg(test)] mod ipc_serialization to commands.rs — 12 tests pinning serde wire shape for every IPC-returned struct (TikiRelease, TikiState, WorkContext/IssueContext, TikiPlan+Phase, DiagnosticsReport+ReleaseCheck, GitHubRelease Some+None, GitHubIssue, GitHubPullRequest, GitHubPrDetail+nested, GitHubComment). Asserts camelCase keys present, skip_serializing_if Some-present/None-absent (GitHubRelease {name,publishedAt,url}; IssueContext optionals both directions), default/non-optional always-present (TikiRelease.archived present when false; DiagnosticsReport.unresolvedScriptPaths [] when empty), WorkContext tag type:"issue"/"release", enum renames (status lowercase, pipelineStep SCREAMING_SNAKE). #259 invariant folded into tiki_release_wire_shape AND original tiki_release_archived_survives_serialization kept. cargo --lib 61→75 (with sibling phase 2's +2); clippy --lib --tests clean. testResults: { framework: "cargo", command: "cargo test --lib", passed: 75, failed: 0, skipped: 0, status: "passed" }.

phases.title: Source-scan guard banning raw Command::new outside hidden_command
phases.content:
Add a guard test asserting `hidden_command` (github/mod.rs) is the ONLY place that constructs a raw `std::process::Command` — so a future spawn can't bypass the Windows CREATE_NO_WINDOW hygiene (#36/#121).

Implement as a cargo test (self-contained in the crate): walk `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/**/*.rs` (use `std::fs` + recursion or `include_str!`-free dir walk; the crate root via `env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")`), read each file, and assert no occurrence of the literal `Command::new(` EXCEPT inside github/mod.rs (the single `hidden_command` definition). Be precise:
- Match `Command::new(` (the construction), not type annotations or comments referencing it — a simple substring scan is acceptable but exclude `//`-comment lines and the github/mod.rs definition line.
- Allow the one legitimate site (github/mod.rs hidden_command). Report file:line of any violation with a message pointing at hidden_command.
- Confirm portable-pty (pty.rs CommandBuilder / native_pty_system) does NOT contain `Command::new(` so it isn't falsely flagged (it uses portable_pty::CommandBuilder, a different type).
- SC3 proof: include an in-test assertion that the matcher WOULD flag a planted bypass — e.g. run the same predicate over a synthetic string `let x = Command::new("gh");` and assert it matches (demonstrates the guard catches a real bypass, not vacuously passing).

Run `cargo test --lib` — green (the real tree has exactly one legitimate site). clippy clean.
verification: cargo guard test scans src/**.rs and passes (only github/mod.rs hidden_command constructs Command::new)
the matcher is shown to flag a planted bypass string (not vacuously green)
phases.summary: Added #[cfg(test)] mod command_hygiene_guard to github/mod.rs — walks src/**/*.rs from env!(CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR)/src, flags any line with literal Command::new( except (a) //-comment lines and (b) tokens inside string literals (odd preceding-quote count), allowlisting only github/mod.rs. Asserts exactly 1 legit site (fails if hidden_command deleted/renamed — non-vacuous). Planted-bypass proof: predicate true for `Command::new("gh")`, false for comment/doc lines. Real tree confirmed: only github/mod.rs L20 (doc comment, excluded) + L25 (hidden_command body) — no bypass. pty.rs uses portable_pty::CommandBuilder, not flagged. cargo --lib green (75 total with phase 1); clippy clean for github/mod.rs. testResults: { framework: "cargo", command: "cargo test --lib", passed: 75, failed: 0, skipped: 0, status: "passed" }.
